edges.description: A subset of Sertoli and Leydig cell tumors are driven by CTNNB1 mutation.
edges.source: Neoplastic Transformation of Gonadal Stromal Cells

edges.description: GNAS and PRKAR1A lesions activate cAMP/PKA signaling in tumor cells.
edges.source: Neoplastic Transformation of Gonadal Stromal Cells

edges.description: In a subset of tumors, dysregulated TGF-beta signaling reprograms Sertoli cells toward a granulosa-like fate, contributing to granulosa-cell tumor formation.
edges.source: Neoplastic Transformation of Gonadal Stromal Cells

edges.description: Tumor cells secrete sex steroids independent of normal regulation.
edges.source: Neoplastic Transformation of Gonadal Stromal Cells

edges.description: Elevated cAMP/PKA signaling drives steroidogenic gene expression in Leydig cell tumors, promoting autonomous sex-steroid synthesis.
Excess estrogen drives gynecomastia and feminization.

nodes.description: Hormonally active Leydig cell tumors autonomously synthesize testosterone; peripheral and intratumoral aromatase converts androgens to estrogens. Excess sex steroids produce gynecomastia in adults and isosexual precocious puberty in prepubertal boys.

evidence.reference_title: A rare cause of hypertestosteronemia in a 68-year-old patient: a Leydig cell tumor due to a somatic GNAS (guanine nucleotide-binding protein, alpha-stimulating activity polypeptide 1)-activating mutation.
evidence.snippet: activating gsp mutation in Leydig cells may result in tumor development, leading to overexpression of the inhibin alpha subunit and hyperactivity of the testosterone biosynthetic pathway.

nodes.description: Tumor-derived estrogen excess produces gynecomastia and can suppress the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, reducing spermatogenesis and fertility.

Testicular sex cord-stromal tumors arise from clonal proliferation of the non-germ-cell gonadal stromal lineages of the testis: steroidogenic Leydig cells of the interstitium, supporting Sertoli cells of the seminiferous tubules, and granulosa-type cells. This distinguishes them from germ cell tumors, which arise from the germ cell lineage.
nodes.id: Neoplastic Transformation of Gonadal Stromal Cells

nodes.description: In a genetically engineered mouse model, sustained Sertoli cell-specific activation of the TGF-beta receptor TGFBR1 is sufficient to reprogram Sertoli cells toward a granulosa-like fate and to drive testicular granulosa cell tumor formation. This provides a mechanistic link between TGF-beta signaling dysregulation and the granulosa-cell phenotype of a subset of testicular sex cord-stromal tumors.

biological_processes: transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Sertoli Cell-Specific Activation of Transforming Growth Factor Beta Receptor 1 Leads to Testicular Granulosa Cell Tumor Formation.
evidence.snippet: overactivation of TGFBR1 in Sertoli cells would promote their transdifferentiation into granulosa-like cells and the formation of TGCTs.

nodes.description: Recurrent activating (exon 3) mutations in CTNNB1 stabilize beta-catenin, leading to constitutive canonical Wnt signaling and nuclear beta-catenin accumulation in a subset of testicular Sertoli cell and Leydig cell tumors.

evidence.reference_title: The role of beta-catenin mutation and SOX9 expression in sex cord-stromal tumours of the testis.
evidence.snippet: β-catenin mutation in SCT results in nuclear β-catenin and cyclin-D1 expressions on immunohistochemical analysis.

nodes.description: Activating GNAS mutations (constitutive Gs-alpha) and loss of the PKA regulatory subunit PRKAR1A (in Carney complex) both increase protein kinase A signaling, driving proliferation and steroidogenesis in Leydig and Sertoli cell tumors. PRKAR1A inactivation underlies large-cell calcifying Sertoli cell tumors in Carney complex.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization of large cell calcifying sertoli cell tumors: A multi-institutional study of 6 benign and 2 malignant tumors.
evidence.snippet: PRKAR1A alterations were present in all cases and appear to be the major driver in LCCSCTs.
